subject: Add option to disable specific deployments
commit_message:
Add option to disable specific deployments

Add option to disable any deployment by using conditions in helm
templates. Each deployment will get helm parameter - boolean flag
allowing to disable it. The flags can be listed in the custom
values.yaml file that can be passed to the createAll script with
'-v' command line option.

Change-Id: I32b795de46c72915c2201c94c23f4e061971bb56
Issue-ID: OOM-380
Signed-off-by: yuryn <Yury.Novitsky@Amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

reviewing - this significant patch will need to be pulled into an env and tested

Patch Set 2:

Hi,
I urge you to review the change and post your comments. You can also get more details on the change here https://jira.onap.org/browse/OOM-380

Patch Set 3:

Can anyone review and commit the change?

Thanks,
Yury.

Patch Set 3:

pulling into a separate master - currently health has an issue (unrelated) OOM-388

Patch Set 3:

Did you consider making better use of YAML structure?  Something like this is much more readable than what is proposed.

myvalues.yaml:

disable:
  aai:
    sparky-be: true
    search-data-service: true
  mso:
    mariadb: true


#{{ if not .Values.disable.aai.sparky-be }}
#{{ if not .Values.disable.mso.mariadb }}

Patch Set 3:

> Did you consider making better use of YAML structure?  Something
 > like this is much more readable than what is proposed.
 > 
 > myvalues.yaml:
 > 
 > disable:
 > aai:
 > sparky-be: true
 > search-data-service: true
 > mso:
 > mariadb: true
 > 
 > 
 > #{{ if not .Values.disable.aai.sparky-be }}
 > #{{ if not .Values.disable.mso.mariadb }}

Yes, I considered it, however it will make the condition in the yaml more complex then just testing a Boolean.

 > yaml more complex then just testing a Boolean.

Also came across this recommendation:
"While structuring data this way is possible, the recommendation is that you keep your values trees shallow, favoring flatness."
At https://docs.helm.sh/chart_template_guide/#values-files
Change has been successfully merged by Mandeep Khinda
